footer.tagline: Discover CLI tools. One shared index for humans and AI agents.
docs.title: How to use AnyCLI
docs.lead: AnyCLI turns CLI tools into structured capability units: humans and agents locate tools by slug, and the site provides install commands and example usage.
docs.agent.title: How AI agents should use it (start here)
docs.agent.desc: Recommended flow: use search to get minimal JSON (including agentHints and install command), then decide whether to install.
docs.agent.commands:
# 1) Exact lookup: slug -> minimal JSON (includes agentHints)
anycli search <slug> --json

# 2) Install: default is dry-run (prints the command, does not run)
anycli install <slug> --json

# 3) Then execute install after confirming
anycli install <slug> --yes --json
docs.agent.prompts.intro: You can paste these prompt templates to an AI agent:
docs.agent.prompts:
Example 1: Install AnyCLI + tool + verify
Please use AnyCLI to install and verify the CLI tool with slug {{slug}}. Follow these steps strictly:

0) Install AnyCLI (if not installed):
   npm i -g @lightcity/anycli
   # After installation, you can run `anycli --version` to confirm

1) Search the tool:
   anycli search {{slug}} --json

2) Review the search result, focusing on agentHints/example_usage:
   - Choose the safest verification command (e.g. --help or --version)

3) Install the tool:
   a) Safety check:
      anycli install {{slug}} --json
      # Confirm install.command is safe
   b) Execute installation:
      anycli install {{slug}} --yes

4) Verify the tool:
   - Run a minimal safe command (e.g. {{binary}} --help or a safe command from example_usage)
   - Summarize the output to confirm the tool works

Prompt 2: commands only, do not execute

Please use AnyCLI to search the CLI tool {{slug}}, but do NOT execute installation. Follow these steps strictly:

0) Confirm AnyCLI is installed:
   npm i -g @lightcity/anycli
   # You may run `anycli --version` to check

1) Search the tool:
   anycli search {{slug}} --json

2) Output:
   - The recommended install command (e.g. anycli install {{slug}} --yes)
   - 1–3 recommended follow-up commands (prefer safe commands in agentHints/example_usage)
   - Prerequisites / cautions (if agentHints.when_to_use mentions auth or config, list them)

⚠️ Output must include commands and notes only. Do NOT attempt to execute the tool.
docs.install.title: Install anycli (for humans & agents)
docs.install.desc: By default it connects to ANYCLI_BASE_URL (defaults to http://localhost:3000).
docs.human.title: For humans
docs.human.desc: If you just want to get started: search first, then use install to print the command (add --yes after confirming).
docs.human.commands:
anycli search <slug>
anycli install <slug>
anycli install <slug> --yes
docs.publish.title: Publishing & maintenance
docs.publish.desc.p1: After signing in, go to the publish page and fill required fields. The site auto-generates agentHints and shows them on the CLI detail page.
about.title: About Datawhale
about.lead: Datawhale is an open-source organization focused on AI, dedicated to building a pure learning community and helping learners grow.
about.who.title: Who we are
about.who.body: We focus on producing and learning AI content: machine learning, deep learning, programming, and math.
about.origin.title: Where we started
about.origin.body: Datawhale began on Dec 6, 2018. The team keeps growing: outstanding students from various universities, as well as engineers from companies like Microsoft, ByteDance, and Baidu.
about.mission.title: What we aim to do
about.mission.body: We bring together ambitious young people to improve how we learn. There are no labels here. If you’re new, we’ll help you learn and change; if you’re advanced, we’ll help you level up and grow.

home.hero.badge: Unified registration & index · For humans and agents
home.hero.p2: We turn CLI tools into structured capability units callable by agents.
home.hero.p3: With consistent metadata and examples, CLI tools become reusable capability entrypoints.
home.featured.title: Featured tools, instant to try
home.featured.desc: Ranked by real-time interest on this site.
home.featured.empty: Run npm run catalog:load to import the curated catalog, or publish your CLI.
home.about.title: Datawhale: an open-source AI learning community
home.about.desc: AnyCLI is built with the Datawhale community. We focus on producing and learning AI content—machine learning, deep learning, programming, and math—building a pure learning circle to help learners grow.

settings.subtitle: Manage your profile.

dashboard.empty: No entries yet. Publish your first tool.
publish.title: Publish a CLI
publish.lead.p1: After submission, an admin review is required before your entry appears publicly. You can also be marked as Featured during review. After submitting, you can open the preview link from
publish.lead.p2: . Published entries can be edited or deleted from the dashboard or detail page.
publish.guide.title: Filling guide
publish.guide.url: Each field has format hints below; URLs must include the protocol (https://).
publish.guide.tagsCats: Add tags/categories one by one using the input + Add (or press Enter).
publish.guide.slugName: The CLI slug is the URL id and cannot be changed after creation. The display name can be anything.
publish.edit.title: Edit CLI
publish.edit.lead: The CLI slug cannot be changed after creation. Featured/GitHub stars keep following the existing rules; publish status stays the same.
publish.error.generic: Submission failed. Please check required fields and URL formats.
publish.field.slug.hint: Used in the URL path (/clis/<slug>). Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, e.g. my-awesome-cli.
publish.field.name: Display name
publish.field.name.hint: The public-facing CLI name; can differ from the slug.
publish.field.description.hint: At least 10 characters. Describe what it does and when to use it so humans and agents can understand.
publish.field.license.hint: Common SPDX identifier. For closed-source, choose Proprietary or Other and fill in.
publish.field.license.customPlaceholder: e.g. EUPL-1.2 or internal license short name
publish.field.urlRequiredHint: Required. Must start with http:// or https://.
publish.field.repository.hint: Required. GitHub/GitLab repo URL for users and star sync.
publish.field.docsUrl.hint: Required. One authoritative entry (README, official docs, usage guide).
publish.field.tags.hint: Add searchable keywords (language, domain, package manager, etc). Avoid duplicates.
publish.field.tags.placeholder: Type then click Add or press Enter
publish.field.tags.itemHint: Keep it short, e.g. npm, python, json
publish.field.categories.hint: Used for site filtering. Prefer lowercase slugs like dev, video, ai (add one by one).
publish.field.installMethod.hint: Choose the primary distribution channel; “Other” is stored as script internally.
publish.field.installPackage.hint: For example: brew formula, npm package, cargo crate name.
publish.field.binaryName.hint: The command you type in the terminal, e.g. gh, ffmpeg.
publish.field.runExample.hint: Required. One-line example for copy/paste and agent reference.
